title: On Seeing Miss Fontenelle In A Favourite Character.
content:
Sweet naivet� of feature,
            Simple, wild, enchanting elf,
        Not to thee, but thanks to nature,
            Thou art acting but thyself.

        Wert thou awkward, stiff, affected,
            Spurning nature, torturing art;
        Loves and graces all rejected,
            Then indeed thou'dst act a part.

    R. B.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "On Seeing Miss Fontenelle In A Favourite Character.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
